response.justification: While the candidate meets the educational requirement (Bachelor's degree + 19 years of experience) and demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, there is a fundamental mismatch with the role's core technical requirements. The position demands expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and AI accelerator performance optimization—areas where the candidate has no demonstrated experience. The candidate's background is primarily in full-stack web development, microservices, and application-level performance optimization, with only recent tangential exposure to AI through building agent infrastructure tooling rather than understanding AI workload characteristics and hardware optimization trade-offs.
strengths: Strong software engineering practices with 19 years of experience across multiple domains
Proven ability to optimize low-latency systems and performance-critical applications (Dunnhumby microservices, D-based async I/O work)
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure and emerging technologies in current role at re:cinq
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience with SoC design, IP modeling, or hardware architecture—core to the role's technical foundation
No background in AI/deep-learning workload analysis, neural network optimization, or GPU/accelerator architecture understanding
Career trajectory shows application and web systems focus; no evidence of hardware-software co-design or performance modeling at the silicon level

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator & hardware optimization expertise
No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
